title: Downloads: Saving from the Internet
drDigitalIntro: When you download something — a PDF, an image, a document — it comes from the internet to your Mac. Click a download link and Safari saves it to your Downloads folder. You can see recent downloads in Safari's sidebar or press Command+Y to open the Downloads panel. From there, you can open the file, show it in Finder, or move it to a folder. Files you download this way are saved to your Mac.
drDigitalSuccess: You know how to download and find files from the internet!
drDigitalHint: Your Downloads folder is where everything from the internet lands.
